text.title: PHPUnit Testing Patterns Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for effective PHPUnit usage.

rule.statement: Every test MUST be structured Arrange-Act-Assert with blank lines separating the three phases. Mixed-up assertions interleaved with setup are forbidden.
rule.rationale: AAA visually communicates intent; reviewers spot logic errors faster.

rule.statement: Test fixtures MUST be built per test via factories (Laravel Factory / data-builder). Shared `setUp()` state across tests is forbidden unless explicitly isolated (DatabaseTransactions trait).
rule.rationale: Shared state creates order-dependence; one test's mutation leaks to the next.

rule.statement: Parameterized tests MUST use `#[DataProvider('methodName')]` (or attribute equivalent). Copy-pasted near-identical tests with one input change are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Data providers compress repetition + give per-row failure messages.

rule.statement: External dependencies MUST be mocked via constructor injection of mock objects. Facade fakes (`Mail::fake()`) MUST be restricted to feature/integration tests, not unit tests.
rule.rationale: DI mocks run without booting Laravel; faster + more isolated.

rule.statement: Tests MUST pass when run in random order (`--order-by=random`). Tests that depend on execution order are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Order dependence hides real bugs and breaks parallel execution.
